topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched in my throat. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in over a decade—the same sneering grin I remembered from the hallways of middle school. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally tapped to view the request. A wave of heat rushed up my neck, making my palms slick. *Seriously?* The thought echoed hollowly in my chest. I quickly minimized the app, shoving my phone into my pocket as if the device itself were suddenly radioactive. I stared intently at a dust mote dancing in a sunbeam, trying to steady the erratic rhythm of my pulse.

[story 2]

When the chime sounded, I froze mid-sip of my lukewarm tea. The screen displayed a familiar name, one that instantly conjured a tightening sensation in my stomach. It was him. The air in the small apartment suddenly felt thick, heavy. I felt a distinct urge to bolt from the chair, to hide under the nearest blanket. My hands started to fidget, twisting the fabric of my sweater into tight, small knots. I reread the message—a simple, 'Hey, long time no see.' My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I took a deep, shaky breath, forcing my gaze back to the screen, my entire body coiled tight with apprehension.

[story 3]

The unexpected ping shattered the monotony of my evening. I glanced at the screen, and the familiar silhouette of the account made my stomach clench with a strange, unpleasant lurch. I chewed on my lower lip until it tasted like dry paper. Every muscle in my shoulders seemed to lock up, preparing for an impact that wasn't physical, but purely psychological. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my living room three times before returning to the sofa. My gaze darted around the room, searching for an escape route, anything to distract from the insistent thumping beneath my ribs. I waited, motionless, for the next inevitable development.
